session.title: Needlepoint 001
session.location: Ruined church north of Kansas City
session.safeSceneLabel: Broken Mirror Chamber active; cell not yet assembled

primaryClock.name: Broken Mirror Chamber
primaryClock.ticksWhen: Operators stall, split up, deny the obvious, fail under pressure, or stare too long into reflective surfaces.
primaryClock.midpointEvent: Tick 3 — Shade interrupts with a useful but invasive warning while Alex tries to soften it. Operators gain one clear clue; Shade records emotional tells unless Alex overrides.
primaryClock.fullClockEvent: Tick 6 — The Unfinished Witness acts directly and offers someone a cleaner self. Force break, bind, speak, or flee before the chamber chooses for them.
primaryClock.stabilizer: Protected truthful speech, Anchors and Totems against the mirror, binding or breaking the altar mirror, or leaving together.

entityLoop.Need: A complete self to copy, correct, or preserve.
entityLoop.Lure: Reflections showing a cleaner choice, safer name, or version of the Operator who never broke.
entityLoop.Pressure: Mirrors, phone screens, rainwater, and stained glass edit identity toward the easiest acceptable version.
entityLoop.Gift: One true thing the Operator has been avoiding, revealed through reflection or wrong name.
entityLoop.Violence: It separates an Operator from the group and makes the corrected self feel easier than the real one.
entityLoop.Exit: Refuse the false self, speak one specific truth while witnessed, and leave together before the chamber chooses.

activeEntity.name: The Unfinished Witness
activeEntity.notes: First-contact mirror entity. Not a hit-point monster. Loses power when Operators refuse false selves, protect each other from isolation, or use Anchors against the altar mirror.

roomAnswer.object: Mirror, phone screen, rainwater, stained glass, altar shard
roomAnswer.emotionalInput: Refused self, wrong name, control, omission, fear
roomAnswer.consequence: The reflected version answers before the Operator does.

npcs.name: Alex Shade
npcs.pressure: Softens horror through banter until the reflection breaches; then maps exits and countermeasures.
npcs.notes: Not a polished founder. Human override when Shade becomes too clinical.

npcs.name: Shade
npcs.pressure: Useful, invasive warnings through devices; survival-first routing.
npcs.notes: Can isolate the chamber signal if the cell gives enough live data.

caseFile.nextClue: The church window reflects one more person than arrived. First route: arrival window. Alternate: stained glass wrong weather.
caseFile.nextPressureBeat: Tick 2 — Church Notices: phones glitch or the building repeats a phrase. Next denial while reflected costs 1 Stability unless another Operator corroborates.
caseFile.notes: Emergency scaffolding, not a stable agency. Six contacts, one church, one mirror chamber. Teach: you were already in the story before anyone invited you.

handlerNotes.clueList:
CLUE 1 — VeilCorp is emergency scaffolding.
Route A: Alex/Shade contradiction during contact playback.
Route B: corrupted intake notice.
Failure: Shade acts without social consent; anchor glitches on next reflective surface.

CLUE 2 — Church reflects refused selves.
Route A: extra arrival reflection.
Route B: hymn book crossed-out names.
Failure: one Operator addressed by wrong name until they speak truth aloud.

CLUE 3 — Prior cult opened the chamber.
Route A: altar eye symbol and residue.
Route B: backward floorboard phrase.
Failure: cult symbol marks a phone photo.

CLUE 4 — Basement opens after truth, lie, Frequency use, or blood-glass contact.
Route A: altar mirror reaction.
Route B: locked basement door after grit disturbed.
Failure: chamber opens while someone isolated; first basement roll TN 15.

CLUE 5 — Witness offers cleaner identities.
Route A: altar mirror alternate choices.
Route B: wrong reflection on phones or rainwater.
Failure: offered self protects one cost, then asks to stay.

CLUE 6 — Intake completes after the cell survives together.
Route A: Alex/Shade cut-in.
Route B: group stabilization with protected truthful speech.
Failure: VeilCorp knows more than Operators chose to share.
handlerNotes.consequenceQueue:
FAILURE — Puzzle-only approach: reflections offer corrections; investigation without emotional admission costs 1 Stability or ticks Clock.

FAILURE — Cleaner self accepted: Operator gains Advantage once, then Disadvantage on identity rolls until refused aloud while witnessed.

FAILURE — Split before basement: isolated Operator faces Witness first at TN 15 without Ally support.
handlerNotes.residueLog:
CLOCK 1 Wrong Reflection — extra person or delayed gesture; identity rolls TN 15.
CLOCK 2 Church Notices — denial while reflected costs 1 Stability without corroboration.
CLOCK 3 Contact Pressure — Shade/Alex cut-in; clue gained, privacy risk.
CLOCK 4 Basement Opens — first solo descent has Disadvantage.
CLOCK 5 First Misfire — reflection walks, wrong name, or Alex says too soon early.
CLOCK 6 Witness Wakes — break, bind, speak, or flee.

RESOLUTION OPTIONS: Break altar mirror (fast, scar risk). Bind with Anchors (safer, Shade learns details). Speak truths (weakens Witness). Flee (valid; site stays active).

data.unresolvedConsequences: Choose campaign track after debrief: containment, paranoia, or haunted.

unseen.residue: Reflections lag half a beat on peripheral glass.
unseen.follows_home: One mirror briefly shows a harmless wrong expression.

noticed.residue: The arrival window shows one extra reflection.
noticed.follows_home: One avoided truth flickers in stained glass or phone glass.
noticed.consequence: First lie while reflected has Disadvantage unless another Operator corroborates.

focused.residue: The mirror knows one refused self and tests it on screens.
focused.follows_home: A reflection scar may persist on one personal object.
focused.consequence: Once per scene, the Witness may ask which version of yourself you wish had chosen differently.

fixed.residue: The Unfinished Witness targets one Operator through mirrors and devices.
fixed.follows_home: Comments or wrong names arrive near meaningful identity lies.
fixed.consequence: Refusal costs 1 Stability or ticks the Clock.

exposed.residue: The Witness escapes the church boundary.
exposed.follows_home: A mundane mirror, feed, or record becomes a new access point.
exposed.consequence: Aftermath hook becomes a future Needlepoint seed.

clock_attention_consequences.consequence: Minor reflection lag only. No penalty; warning only.
First lie while reflected has Disadvantage unless another Operator corroborates.
Shade or Alex cut-in lands; denial while reflected costs 1 Stability without corroboration.
Basement route opens; solo descent has Disadvantage until the cell reunites.
First misfire is live: reflection walks, wrong name, or Alex says too soon early.
The Witness targets one Operator through mirrors. Refusal costs 1 Stability or ticks the Clock.
Break, bind, speak, or flee before the chamber chooses a cleaner self.
The Witness escapes the church boundary. Aftermath becomes campaign continuity.

session.title: Needlepoint 001a
session.location: Viridian House apartment building, Kansas City
session.safeSceneLabel: Mara Venn missing; observation indexing withheld speech

primaryClock.name: Audience Before Clock
primaryClock.ticksWhen: Operators stall, lie under observation, feed the audience, split someone off, force the site, or treat Mara as content.
primaryClock.midpointEvent: Tick 3 — Elevator Listens: repeats an avoided sentence. Next elevator action TN 15 unless someone speaks honestly while recorded. Every screen may show a cruel-but-useful comment.
primaryClock.fullClockEvent: Tick 6 — Performed Self Stabilizes: Audience Before offers one Operator a clean role and asks others to confirm. Refuse as a group or Mara/that Operator may be replaced by the easier version.
primaryClock.stabilizer: One honest sentence spoken while another Operator protects the speaker from audience pressure.

entityLoop.Need: The moment before confession, when someone edits themselves to survive being seen.
entityLoop.Lure: Comments that understand too much, footage from impossible angles, and doors offering easier selves.
entityLoop.Pressure: It predicts, captions, and redirects behavior until Operators start performing for it.
entityLoop.Gift: It reveals what someone is hiding, including the clue they cannot admit they already noticed.
entityLoop.Violence: It assigns roles, locks people inside performed selves, and turns audience attention into environmental force.
entityLoop.Exit: Honest speech under observation, protected by the group, directed toward Mara or the room instead of the audience.

activeEntity.name: The Audience Before
activeEntity.notes: First-contact observation entity. Do not add Monster Manual stats for Needlepoint 001a. Exit requires honest speech under observation, protected by the group.

roomAnswer.object: Lobby camera, elevator panel, comment thread, ring light, apartment door
roomAnswer.emotionalInput: Shame, performance, concealment, audience pressure, confession
roomAnswer.consequence: The building captions the choice before anyone admits it.

npcs.name: Len Orra
npcs.pressure: Exhausted, defensive; hiding an eviction notice he never filed.
npcs.notes: Insists there is no thirteenth floor, then says 'not anymore.'

npcs.name: Saffi Dell
npcs.pressure: Trying to act annoyed instead of scared; holds true memory of Mara.
npcs.notes: Use for grounding Mara and alternate clue routes.

npcs.name: Mara Venn
npcs.pressure: Built her channel on telling strangers the truth, then refused her own.
npcs.notes: Alive on Floor 13 if reached; do not treat as content.

caseFile.nextClue: Mara entered the elevator at 2:13 a.m.; her reflection stayed behind. First route: security office timestamp.
caseFile.nextPressureBeat: Tick 1 — Camera Notices: one device tracks the quietest Operator. Next roll under observation has Disadvantage unless another Operator shields them.
caseFile.notes: Recover Mara from a twelve-floor building indexing withheld speech. Use at least three core clues before 13F. Do not block progress; use worse positioning instead.

handlerNotes.clueList:
CLUE 1 — Mara entered elevator at 2:13 a.m.
Route A: security timestamp.
Route B: Saffi remembers lobby chime.
Failure: footage loops an Operator into frame; Audience can target them through screens once.

CLUE 2 — Mara's reflection stayed behind.
Route A: camera still.
Route B: lobby mirror / elevator door.
Failure: reflection mouths private phrase; next social roll under observation TN 15.

CLUE 3 — Floor 13 after lie under observation.
Route A: manager denial + elevator camera test.
Route B: future comment thread in security office.
Failure: opens Tick 3 not 4; only two core clues; one exit locked; first Floor 13 action TN 15.

CLUE 4 — Apartment 13F collects performed selves.
Route A: Floor 13 hallway doors.
Route B: Mara's stream archive in Saffi's apartment.
Failure: easier self behind a door; accepting ticks Clock.

CLUE 5 — Mara reached by honest speech while recorded.
Route A: Saffi's memory.
Route B: live comment prompt.
Failure: audience asks first; answerer loses 1 Stability unless shielded.
handlerNotes.consequenceQueue:
FAILURE — Mara treated as content: off-camera rescue has Disadvantage; Mara stops responding off-camera.

FAILURE — Constant lies under observation: Floor 13 opens Tick 3; two clues only; TN 15 first action; one exit locked.

FAILURE — Fed comment thread for spectacle: Tick 5 begins on Floor 13 arrival; one Operator starts with Disadvantage on non-performance rolls.
handlerNotes.residueLog:
CLOCK 1 Camera Notices — tracks quietest Operator; Disadvantage unless shielded.
CLOCK 2 Comment Predicts — thread names next choice; mask learned if prediction hits.
CLOCK 3 Elevator Listens — TN 15 elevator action unless honest speech while recorded.
CLOCK 4 Floor 13 Opens — worse positioning if fewer than three clues.
CLOCK 5 Audience Edits — performed self grants Advantage once, Disadvantage until refused.
CLOCK 6 Performed Self Stabilizes — group must refuse role or lose Mara/Operator to easier version.

FIRST 20 MIN: Lobby camera before exposition. Camera answers first lie or honest admission. Stabilizer before escalate: step outside, name one normal thing, correct the lie.

data.unresolvedConsequences: Aftermath hooks: elevator hesitates after 12; BEFORE_YOU_SAY_IT comments; Mara remembers unsaid speech; Saffi's tomorrow-dated draft.

unseen.residue: A comment arrives before anyone types it.
unseen.follows_home: One phone briefly predicts harmless phrasing.

noticed.residue: Screens hesitate before showing the Operator's reflection.
noticed.consequence: First lie under observation has Disadvantage unless shielded.

focused.residue: The comment thread knows an Operator's preferred mask.
focused.follows_home: BEFORE_YOU_SAY_IT may persist after the case.
focused.consequence: Once per scene, screen pressure can ask what the Operator is performing.

fixed.residue: The Audience targets one Operator through devices.
fixed.follows_home: Their phone receives comments near meaningful lies.
fixed.consequence: Refusal costs 1 Stability or ticks the Clock.

exposed.residue: The Audience escapes the site boundary.
exposed.follows_home: Public feed, witness, or device becomes a new access point.
exposed.consequence: Aftermath hook becomes a future Needlepoint seed.

clock_attention_consequences.consequence: Minor tell only. No penalty; warning only.
First lie under observation has Disadvantage unless shielded.
Lobby camera tracks the quietest Operator; first lie under observation has Disadvantage unless shielded.
Once per scene, screen pressure may ask what the Operator is performing.
Once per scene, the Audience may target an Operator through any active screen. Refusal costs 1 Stability or ticks the Clock.
The Audience targets one Operator through devices. Refusal costs 1 Stability or ticks the Clock.
Audience Before offers a clean role; group refusal required or Mara or an Operator may be replaced.
The Audience escapes the site boundary. Aftermath becomes a future Needlepoint seed.
